← Bioregional Organizations

🟡 Koalisi Ekonomi Membumi

Bioregional Organizer
🌐 Visit website

A collective movement catalyzing sustainable investment ecosystems across Indonesia through their Value Chain Collaboration Canvas approach. KEM connects local governments, smallholder farmers, businesses, and financial institutions to build nature-based commodity value chains.

Bioregional Context

Bali is a volcanic island in the Indonesian archipelago known for its forested highlands, terraced rice fields, and coastal areas. The island faces critical environmental challenges including water scarcity, with tourism-intensive areas experiencing severe deficits.

Place
Bali, Indonesia
links
https://www.canva.com/design/DAGZcco_p_s/SGmpZwnbMJKz-03C8c8GgQ/edit

Tags

🪢 Network_Weaving ⛓️ Value_Chain_Development 🌾 Regenerative_Agriculture 💧 Watershed_Repair 🔘 Governance_Bodies 📐 Participatory_Planning_and_Strategies ♾️ Commons_Governance_Protocols

Background

Bali combines rich forest endowment with acute resource pressure. Forests cover more than 249,000 hectares; Buleleng, Jembrana, and Tabanan hold 63% of tree cover. Those forests underpin high-value crops—vanilla, cacao, coffee, spices—and buffer biodiversity and livelihoods.

Tourism growth and land conversion are squeezing water supplies. In 2021, the sharpest deficits appeared in tourism-heavy districts with over 250 lodging businesses each. Keeping forest-rich regencies such as Jembrana and Tabanan from wholesale conversion to resorts is part of island-wide water security.

Origins and Network

Koalisi Ekonomi Membumi (KEM) organises as a movement to speed up investment that fits Indonesian place-based wisdom. It takes Tri Hita Karana—“three causes of prosperity”: harmony with spirit, among people, and with nature—as a governing ethic for weaving spiritual, social, and ecological aims in bioregional work.

Members such as Supernova Ecosystem, Katalys, Swadaya, Pandegha, and PBF host collaboration and delivery. KEM works in Bali, West Kalimantan, Central Sulawesi, and South Sumatra, linking districts, enterprises, and finance through the Value Chain Collaboration Canvas (VC3).

Activities

🔸

Value Chain Collaboration Canvas

 🔸

100 Regenerative Village Program

 🔸

Multi-Stakeholder Forest Governance

 🔸

Planning Assistance to Local Governments

Vision and Legacy

KEM seeks proof-of-concept bioeconomy and nature-based value chains that reduce dependence on extractive industry and industrial monoculture: 100 jurisdictions committed to sustainable development, 100+ nature-based enterprises, connected chains, and about USD 200 million in funding and transactions. By May 2023, roughly USD 35 million was unlocked, toward a USD 50 million target by 2025 (rolling disbursement).

The bet is that Tri Hita Karana-style alignment—people, nature, and policy—can show how forest water, farmer income, and visitor economies co-evolve at scale across Indonesia.
